title: Rumpelstiltskin
lines: Once there was a poor miller who boasted to the king that his daughter could spin straw into gold.
The king, thinking this was true, locked the girl in a room full of straw with a spinning wheel.
He ordered her to spin it all into gold by morning, threatening to have her executed if she did not.
The girl wept because she did not know what to do.
Suddenly a strange little man appeared in the room.
He offered to spin the straw into gold for her if she would give him her necklace.
She agreed, and he spun all the straw into beautiful gold.
The king was amazed and delighted but wanted even more gold.
He locked her in an even larger room full of straw.
Again the little man appeared and offered to spin the straw into gold in exchange for her ring.
Again she agreed, and again he succeeded.
The king was still not satisfied and locked her in a third room, even larger than the first two.
He said if she could spin this straw into gold, she would become his wife.
Again the little man came and offered to help, but this time he asked for her firstborn child.
Desperate, she agreed.
He spun the straw into gold, and the king married her.
A year later the queen gave birth to a beautiful son.
The little man came to claim the child.
The queen was devastated and begged him to take anything else instead.
He offered her a chance: if she could guess his name within three days, she would keep her child.
The queen sent messengers throughout the kingdom to find out the little man's name.
On the third day, one of her messengers returned with news of a strange little man dancing by a fire in the forest, singing, 'Rumpelstiltskin is my name!'
When the little man came the next day, the queen guessed correctly, and he was so angry that he tore himself in two and vanished.
The queen and her son were happy and safe for all their days.
